reports.remarks: EF0 confirmed tornado. Estimated max wind 85 mph.
EF1 tornado. Max estimated winds 95 mph.
Trees came down across Route 27 just north of the county line.

An EF1 tornado touched down south of Grand Valley during the early evening of June 14. The path length was a little over half a mile, with a maximum path width of around 150 yards. Maximum winds were estimated to have been around 95 mph. Damage was limited to trees being snapped and uprooted along the tornado path in Southwest Township.

An NWS Cleveland survey confirmed a brief EF-1 tornado touched down in central Crawford County at 4:28 PM EDT. Tracking southeast from Dibble Hill and Theuret Hill Roads, it reached peak winds of 90 mph and a width of 75 yards. Damage included snapped limbs, uprooted trees on a garage at Stoltz Road, and a snapped power pole. Rotational wind evidence, including scattered debris, was noted on east side of local structures.

An NWS Cleveland survey confirmed a brief EF-1 tornado touched down in central Crawford County tracked across Leimbach Road, peak winds of 90 mph and a width of 50 yards. Damage included snapped and uprooted hardwood trees, with one tree damaging a detached garage. The debris pattern confirmed a tornadic wind field before the tornado dissipated along its short, intermittent path.

Corrects previous tornado report from 2 S Pine Glen. An NWS Storm Survey team confirmed an EF1 Tornado near Pine Glen in Centre County. The survey team noted a considerable number of trees down along with a roof blown off of a home south of the town. Maximum peak winds of 110 mph were estimated along a 1.16 mile path.

An NWS Storm Survey team confirmed an EF0 Tornado near Pine Glen in Centre County. The survey team noted a considerable number of trees down along with a roof blown off of a home south of the town. Maximum peak winds of 110 mph were estimated along a 1.16 mile path.
An NWS Storm Survey team confirmed an EF0 Tornado in Homer Township, along State Route 872. Maximum peak winds of 75 mph were observed along a 0.01 mile path.
